jokes.text: I'm currently catfishing Anthony Kiedis.
jokes.setup: April explains how she got Red Hot Chili Peppers to donate a guitar
jokes.punchline: Reveals she's deceiving the lead singer romantically to get charity donations
jokes.explanation: Perfect April line - morally questionable scheme delivered matter-of-factly. Strong character integration and economy.

jokes.text: if I'm gonna perform Islands In the Stream with a Sacagawea hologram
jokes.setup: Leslie explains what they need money for at the unity concert
jokes.punchline: The bizarre specific combination of Kenny Rogers song with historical figure hologram
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Leslie's grandiose, historically-obsessed planning style with an absurd specific detail.

jokes.text: That's exactly what I told Kyle when he told me his wife was cheating on him.
jokes.setup: Andy admits he's not good at keeping secrets
jokes.punchline: Immediately proves it by revealing someone else's secret
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy - proves the point by contradicting it immediately. Good structure and character work.

jokes.text: My neighbor Eric-- he's kept a secret for 20 years. He's in Witness Protection.
jokes.setup: Andy tries to prove people can keep secrets
jokes.punchline: Reveals his neighbor's biggest secret while making the point
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of Andy's inability to keep secrets. The irony is perfectly structured.

jokes.text: [Shouts] What? Ron Swanson asking for help?
jokes.setup: Ron asks Donna for a favor
jokes.punchline: Donna's shocked outburst at Ron breaking character
jokes.explanation: Good character moment playing on Ron's independence, though somewhat predictable.

jokes.text: Keep your voice down, woman!
jokes.setup: Donna shouts about Ron asking for help
jokes.punchline: Ron's embarrassed, angry response to being called out
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up showing Ron's discomfort with vulnerability. Solid character work.

jokes.text: Tammy two once seduced a coroner and had me declared legally dead just so she could get a discount on a plane ticket.
jokes.setup: Ron explains why his ex-wives are the worst people in the world
jokes.punchline: Incredibly elaborate scheme for a minor financial benefit
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - starts with 'worst people' and delivers something genuinely insane. Perfect Tammy callback with new absurd detail.

jokes.text: Think they're valuable? Cover me.
jokes.setup: Leslie considers auctioning off fish
jokes.punchline: She's about to do something ridiculous involving the fish that requires backup
jokes.explanation: Visual gag setup showing Leslie's manic energy. Economy is good but not highly original.

jokes.text: You're stressing out for two now.
jokes.setup: Ben tells Leslie to take it easy during pregnancy
jokes.punchline: Play on 'eating for two' but with stress instead
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay that fits the situation. Not groundbreaking but functional and economical.

jokes.text: That's actually a pretty decent t-shirt idea.
jokes.setup: Ben makes pregnancy stress joke
jokes.punchline: Leslie immediately thinks of merchandising it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie response - turning a personal moment into a potential civic project. Good character integration.

jokes.text: Come on, you two. Let's saddle you up and put some jelly on the belly.
jokes.setup: Doctor greets pregnant couple
jokes.punchline: Uncomfortably casual, overly familiar medical talk
jokes.explanation: Establishes Dr. Saperstein's inappropriately casual bedside manner. Mild cringe comedy.

jokes.text: Oh, I forgot, other hand. Crap.
jokes.setup: Andy checks his schedule written on his hands
jokes.punchline: He forgot to check both hands for his appointments
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy physical comedy showing his simple but flawed organizational system. Good character work.

jokes.text: Oh, I forgot, other hand. Crap.
jokes.setup: Andy checks his schedule
jokes.punchline: Reveals he writes on both hands as organization system
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy - childlike organization method. Good physical comedy element.

jokes.text: You get twice the secrets.
jokes.setup: April explains why Andy should tell her his secret
jokes.punchline: Treating secrets like a mathematical commodity
jokes.explanation: Perfect April logic - reducing marriage to a transactional secret-sharing arrangement. Excellent economy.

jokes.text: I'm not acting weird. You're acting weird.
jokes.setup: April calls out Andy's weird behavior
jokes.punchline: Classic defensive reversal while clearly acting weird
jokes.explanation: Standard deflection joke. Works for character but not particularly original or memorable.

jokes.text: [Breathing heavily] [Saw whirring]
jokes.setup: Andy claims he needs to get back to work
jokes.punchline: Cut to him doing manual labor, stress-breathing
jokes.explanation: Visual comedy of Andy's stress manifesting in frantic work activity.

jokes.text: I'm getting a picture of you volunteering at a public elementary school in case I ever need to blackmail you.
jokes.setup: Donna takes a photo of Ron with children
jokes.punchline: Treating Ron's kindness as compromising material
jokes.explanation: Brilliant inversion - Ron's character growth is so against type it could be used for blackmail. Perfect character understanding.

jokes.text: I am Donna's work-proximity associate.
jokes.setup: Joe asks about Ron's relationship to Donna
jokes.punchline: Ron's deliberately cold description of their friendship
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron - technically accurate but emotionally distant description. Great character voice and economy.

jokes.text: The baby has two bodies?
jokes.setup: Doctor reveals there's another baby on the ultrasound
jokes.punchline: Ben's confused interpretation of what twins means
jokes.explanation: Good panic response showing Ben's overwhelmed state. Simple but effective confusion humor.

jokes.text: And here's a fourth. I'm so sorry, no. It's a little fleck of cream cheese on the screen.
jokes.setup: Doctor keeps finding more babies on ultrasound
jokes.punchline: False alarm - it's just food on the equipment
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection and escalation. The mundane explanation after the emotional peak is perfectly timed.

jokes.text: Wow, you guys really dodged a bullet. Can you imagine raising four children at one time? Ha, a nightmare.
jokes.setup: Doctor says they avoided having quadruplets
jokes.punchline: Complete lack of awareness that triplets are still overwhelming
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing the doctor's obliviousness. Minor cringe comedy from poor bedside manner.

jokes.text: If we do this right, we can be parents to 1/3 of the supreme court!
jokes.setup: Leslie gets excited about having triplets
jokes.punchline: Immediately planning their political careers
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie - turns personal moment into civic planning. Excellent character integration and ambitious absurdity.

jokes.text: a going-out-of-business sale
jokes.setup: Doctor explains why older women have more multiple births
jokes.punchline: Comparing reproductive system to retail clearance
jokes.explanation: Hilariously inappropriate medical analogy. Good cringe humor with strong economy and memorable phrasing.

jokes.text: Leslie, I need you to be Buddha on quaaludes, and, Ben, you are Matthew McConaughey in a hammock on the beach.
jokes.setup: Doctor prescribes relaxation for the couple
jokes.punchline: Incredibly specific and bizarre relaxation metaphors
jokes.explanation: Brilliant specificity in the relaxation advice. The McConaughey reference is perfectly timed and absurd.

jokes.text: All right, all right. All right.
jokes.setup: Doctor references Matthew McConaughey
jokes.punchline: Ben immediately does McConaughey impression
jokes.explanation: Perfect immediate callback. Not original but excellently timed and economical.

jokes.text: I will be there in two flaps of a butterfly's wing, and I love you.
jokes.setup: Ben tries to be relaxed after doctor's advice
jokes.punchline: Overly poetic, forced zen language
jokes.explanation: Good showing of Ben's attempt to follow medical advice by being artificially calm. Mild cringe from the overcompensation.

jokes.text: We are so [bleep] screwed.
jokes.setup: Ben just expressed zen-like calm
jokes.punchline: Leslie immediately contradicts with panicked profanity
jokes.explanation: Excellent juxtaposition with Ben's forced calm. Perfect economy and timing.

jokes.text: Look, I hate it when my students are upset, so I just went out, driving around. I checked a few neighborhood parks. It took barely six hours.
jokes.setup: Joe explains how he found a student's lost dog
jokes.punchline: Reveals the extreme time commitment with casual delivery
jokes.explanation: Good character establishment - Joe as excessively helpful to the point of being overwhelming.

jokes.text: It's the best muffin I've ever had.
jokes.setup: Joe does elaborate favors for students and parents
jokes.punchline: Parent's effusive praise for his homemade muffins
jokes.explanation: Part of building Joe as the 'perfect' guy. Functional setup for Donna's frustration but not particularly funny alone.

jokes.text: I got so many kids from different women, I just get all of their birthdays out of the way one day a year.
jokes.setup: Someone asks whose birthday party they're at
jokes.punchline: Craig's efficiency approach to multiple baby mama obligations
jokes.explanation: Dark but efficient logic that fits Craig perfectly. Good economy and character-specific humor.

jokes.text: Also, tell me your secret.
jokes.setup: April gives Andy logistical information about his schedule
jokes.punchline: Seamlessly transitions back to demanding the secret
jokes.explanation: Good running gag - April's persistent focus on the secret mixed with practical business.

jokes.text: It's about Larry. He is very sick. He has some kind of disease.
jokes.setup: Andy finally reveals his secret under pressure
jokes.punchline: Makes up a fake story about Larry being sick
jokes.explanation: Good Andy panic lie - creates fake dramatic story to avoid real secret.

jokes.text: Oh, so that's why you've been so nice to him and let him carry all your stuff.
jokes.setup: Andy claims Larry is sick
jokes.punchline: April logically connects this to Andy's recent behavior
jokes.explanation: Good April logic - finding patterns where none exist, enabling Andy's lie.

jokes.text: It's about Larry. He is very sick. He has some kind of disease.
jokes.setup: Andy finally reveals his 'secret' to April
jokes.punchline: Completely made up story about Larry being sick
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing Andy creating lies to avoid revealing the real secret. Character-appropriate behavior.

jokes.text: A very wealthy and elegant-looking woman with a diamond-encrusted eye patch just raised her hand.
jokes.setup: Leslie tries to drum up auction bids
jokes.punchline: Completely fictional bidder with elaborate description
jokes.explanation: Creative desperation from Leslie. The specific detail of 'diamond-encrusted eye patch' is memorably absurd.

jokes.text: The sheik has thrown his hat into the ring.
jokes.setup: Leslie continues inventing bidders
jokes.punchline: Even more elaborate fictional international bidder
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the fictional bidder concept. Works as part of the sequence.

jokes.text: I am hanging on by a thread over here.
jokes.setup: Andy begs Ben to let him tell April about the pregnancy
jokes.punchline: Admits he's barely maintaining the secret
jokes.explanation: Good Andy desperation - builds the running gag of his inability to keep secrets.

jokes.text: I didn't mean to say 'Bye.'
jokes.setup: Andy hangs up the phone after saying 'Okay'
jokes.punchline: Realizes he said the wrong goodbye
jokes.explanation: Good Andy moment - his stress causing basic social confusion.

jokes.text: the gentleman with the crazy hat and a monkey on his shoulder
jokes.setup: Leslie continues the fake bidder charade
jokes.punchline: Most elaborate fictional bidder yet
jokes.explanation: Part of the escalating pattern but getting repetitive. Still fits Leslie's character.

jokes.text: 3,000, says the gentleman with the crazy hat and a monkey on his shoulder.
jokes.setup: Leslie's fictional bidders continue
jokes.punchline: Even more outlandish bidder description
jokes.explanation: Continues the escalation with increasingly absurd fictional bidder details.

jokes.text: I was licking icing off of my finger, and then, boom, I swallow my wedding ring. And I wish I could say it was the first time.
jokes.setup: Larry explains his 'embarrassing' situation
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's repeatedly swallowed his wedding ring
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry moment - seemingly innocent setup with escalating embarrassing detail. Good character work.

jokes.text: I've gotten very good at passing it. It's just a matter of getting out the old colander.
jokes.setup: Larry admits to repeatedly swallowing his ring
jokes.punchline: TMI about his retrieval process
jokes.explanation: Excellent cringe escalation. The colander detail is both specific and disturbing. Classic Larry overshare.

jokes.text: covering everything from the culture of Southern California to drug use in Southern California
jokes.setup: Ben promotes Red Hot Chili Peppers guitar
jokes.punchline: Their musical range is actually very narrow
jokes.explanation: Brilliant deadpan observation about RHCP's limited thematic range. Perfect economy and structure.

jokes.text: I'm more of a Jack Johnson guy.
jokes.setup: Herman explains why he erased the signatures
jokes.punchline: Prefers a completely different style of music
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - the musical taste clash explains the destruction.

jokes.text: It's not even the original lineup.
jokes.setup: Herman defends erasing the signatures
jokes.punchline: Adds insult about band authenticity
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - not only wrong style but also inauthentic version of wrong style.

jokes.text: You have very weak hands.
jokes.setup: After Ben gets injured in guitar struggle
jokes.punchline: Unnecessarily harsh assessment of Ben's physical strength
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Herman's blunt nature. Economy is excellent, adds insult to injury.

jokes.text: if flea had sent you his bass, you could've lost a thumb
jokes.setup: EMT assesses Ben's guitar injury
jokes.punchline: Weirdly specific knowledge about bass weight vs guitar
jokes.explanation: Funny specific detail about instrument weights. Good absurdist touch from unexpected character.

jokes.text: He's constantly being respectful, wanting to spend time with me, trying to 'connect' with me. It's a nightmare. I become boring.
jokes.setup: Donna explains why Joe is her 'Tammy'
jokes.punchline: Lists genuinely positive traits as relationship problems
jokes.explanation: Good Donna character insight - fear of healthy relationship changing her personality.

jokes.text: unless you choose the right grail. It's the dusty one.
jokes.setup: Larry responds to comment about everyone dying
jokes.punchline: Casual Indiana Jones reference as practical advice
jokes.explanation: Good Larry moment - treating movie logic as real advice. Decent pop culture reference with character spin.

jokes.text: Ben is dying--he has the same disease Larry has. Leslie is dying. They're both dying. I'm dying.
jokes.setup: Andy tries multiple fake explanations for his behavior
jokes.punchline: Escalates to everyone dying including himself
jokes.explanation: Good Andy panic escalation - when one lie fails, he multiplies the problem.

jokes.text: They're moving again to China...town in France.
jokes.setup: Andy tries another fake secret about Chris and Ann
jokes.punchline: Absurd geographic combination that makes no sense
jokes.explanation: Good Andy logic - combining places in a way that sounds plausible but isn't. Character-appropriate confusion.

jokes.text: Tom has something wrong with his butt.
jokes.setup: Andy's final desperate lie attempt
jokes.punchline: Settles on crude medical problem for Tom
jokes.explanation: Andy's desperation leads to juvenile medical humor. Weakest of his lie attempts.

jokes.text: I promise not to cheat on you if you promise not to try to pin me down too fast.
jokes.setup: Donna agrees to dinner with Joe
jokes.punchline: Sets boundary that sounds like avoiding commitment
jokes.explanation: Good Donna character moment - attempting to maintain independence while entering relationship.

jokes.text: I'll wear that red thing when you deserve to see me in that red thing.
jokes.setup: Joe asks Donna to wear a specific outfit
jokes.punchline: Donna's confident, controlling response
jokes.explanation: Perfect Donna power move. Strong character voice and confident delivery.

jokes.text: the Indiana scholarship for pretty blondes who like to read. It's now called the Virginia Woolf prize--different time.
jokes.setup: Leslie explains how her kids will get scholarships
jokes.punchline: Reveals she got scholarship for superficial qualities, not merit
jokes.explanation: Great Leslie moment showing her privilege and the era she grew up in. Good social commentary with humor.

jokes.text: You just look so crazy right now, but cute.
jokes.setup: Ben is having a financial stress breakdown
jokes.punchline: Leslie finds his panic endearing rather than concerning
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Leslie's love for Ben even in crisis. Sweet and funny dynamic.

jokes.text: You just look so crazy right now, but cute.
jokes.setup: Ben is having a stress breakdown
jokes.punchline: Leslie finds his panic endearing
jokes.explanation: Good Leslie reaction - finding attraction in chaos. Sweet but funny.

jokes.text: I am immune to stress because I have you.
jokes.setup: Ben asks why Leslie is calm when she's usually stressed
jokes.punchline: Sweet romantic explanation for her calmness
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment that inverts expectations. Not laugh-out-loud funny but emotionally effective.

jokes.text: You're King Sparkle of Cupcake Forest. It suits you.
jokes.setup: Donna continues her assessment of Ron
jokes.punchline: Uses the ridiculous title as evidence of his growth
jokes.explanation: Great callback that reinforces the absurd image. Good character observation.

jokes.text: Your family has made you a more patient and empathetic person. You take that back.
jokes.setup: Donna compliments Ron's character growth
jokes.punchline: Ron's horrified rejection of being called empathetic
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron response treating empathy like an insult. Excellent character work and structure.

jokes.text: You're King Sparkle of Cupcake Forest. It suits you.
jokes.setup: Donna points out Ron's character growth
jokes.punchline: The absurd visual of tough Ron in a glittery crown
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual juxtaposition of Ron's masculinity with childish fantasy play. Great character development moment.

jokes.text: Andy, the camo is not working. I can see you.
jokes.setup: Andy tries to hide from April in camouflage
jokes.punchline: April's matter-of-fact observation of his failed stealth
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy moment. Andy's childish hiding attempt meets April's directness.

jokes.text: I'm sure you will both raise a wonderful child with whom I will profoundly disagree on nearly everything.
jokes.setup: Ron congratulates Leslie and Ben on pregnancy
jokes.punchline: Backhanded congratulations acknowledging ideological differences
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron - genuine affection wrapped in political disagreement. Excellent character voice and structure.

jokes.text: First my cousin Winona gets into a car accident the night my one-man show opens, and now this? Why are my accomplishments always overshadowed?
jokes.setup: Everyone celebrates Leslie's pregnancy news
jokes.punchline: Craig makes it about himself and his theatrical career
jokes.explanation: Perfect Craig narcissism - turning pregnancy announcement into personal grievance about his art. Good character consistency.
